connectors.purpose: Primary output standard — defines the structure, tone, and rules for every section of the game page. Every paragraph must conform to this before submission.

Defines the publishing lifecycle this skill follows. Phases 1–7 map to the execution flow: keyword and SERP research, content planning, writing, technical, on-page SEO, publishing, and post-publish. Compliance with EEAT is a hard requirement.
Keeps the page audience-aware. Every section should be written for the Metric Gamer reader — the player who wants peer-validated context before buying, not a critic's verdict.

Provides the question pool for the FAQ section. Universal questions apply to every game; conditional categories apply where the game qualifies. Templates define format rules (second person, 3 sentences max, lead negative with 'no').
Background reference for how metrics, rubrics, and scores relate. Informs which genre metric set to select and how to interpret rubric levels when assigning scores.

dynamic_connectors.note: Load the author persona file that matches the 'author' input before writing. This is required — the author persona defines voice, vocabulary, what the author notices, and how they close each section.
